title: Persist market cooldown across restarts

context.problem_statement: Tonight's approved audit found that _market_cooldown is written in the close path but stored only in memory. Restarts wipe it, allowing markets to reopen before their intended cooldown expiry.
context.manager_intent: Implement the smallest safe persistence path so market cooldown expiry timestamps survive restart and are reloaded on startup.

objective: Write _market_cooldown expiry timestamps into the runtime state path and reload them on startup so cooldown protection survives restart.
conditional_allowed_files: C:\Users\johnny\Desktop\sports_bot_v2\runtime\state.json write/read path only as implemented through existing code in bot_core.py or a tiny directly related state helper if strictly required
forbidden_actions: Read every file in the repo
Manual edits to runtime/state.json outside the code path
required_work: Inspect where _market_cooldown is written in the close path
Inspect the existing state write path and startup load path in bot_core.py
Add the smallest safe persistence so cooldown expiry timestamps are included in state output and reloaded on startup
Preserve existing cooldown semantics; only add persistence/reload behavior
Keep the patch local and minimal
acceptance_criteria: _market_cooldown expiry timestamps are persisted through the runtime state path
Cooldown data is reloaded on startup
Restart no longer wipes active cooldowns
No files outside allowed scope are modified unless explicitly documented under conditional scope
verification_required: Explain old behavior vs new behavior
Show where cooldown is written and where it is reloaded
Run py_compile on each changed Python file
State whether restart is required for the fix to take effect
